action.reasoning: Observation: exprate improved to 0.0892 (val peak 0.1065) after fixes; debug shows correct simple expr (sqrt{48}, 26, qt=2q, 4^2+...) but failures on complex (derivatives, long nums, sub/superscripts). Loss ~1.01 plateau. Analysis: ResNet+attention+aspect-pad+aug learns structure but greedy decode + exposure bias cause symbol/seq errors; current generate works but beam search can recover better paths for exact match (typical +3-8% in HMER). Code now stable (no truncation, full train completed). Decision: Add complete beam search (width=5, length norm) to Decoder.generate (fallback to greedy for bs>1, single-sample for beam); update MathExprRecognizer wrapper; set beam=5 in predict/debug (bs=1 in predict for simplicity); run debug.py to verify better preds (e.g. longer nums, cleaner fractions), then submit_eval on best.pt for immediate gain without retraining (fits tight ~35min budget). Expect test exprate >0.11.
